name: 阿诺德·勋伯格
brief: 勋伯格出生于维也纳一个犹太家庭，几乎完全自学成才。他的早期作品（如《升华之夜》）延续了瓦格纳的晚期浪漫风格，随后逐渐走向无调性。1920年代他发明了十二音技法，成为20世纪音乐最重要的理论贡献。纳粹上台后他流亡美国，在洛杉矶度过了余生。他患有严重的「十三恐惧症」——讽刺的是，他于1951年7月13日（星期五）去世，终年76岁（7+6=13）。
briefEn: Schoenberg was born into a Jewish family in Vienna and was largely self-taught. His early works, such as Verklärte Nacht, continued the late-Romantic idiom of Wagner before he gradually moved toward atonality. In the 1920s he invented the twelve-tone technique, arguably the most consequential theoretical innovation in twentieth-century music. After the Nazis came to power he emigrated to the United States and spent the rest of his life in Los Angeles. He suffered from severe triskaidekaphobia — in a grim irony, he died on Friday the 13th of July 1951, at the age of seventy-six (7 + 6 = 13).
contribution: 勋伯格是调性瓦解后最重要的建设者。当他意识到传统调性和声已经耗尽其可能性后，他没有停留在自由无调性的混沌中，而是发明了十二音技法（Twelve-tone Technique）——将一个八度中的12个半音排成一个序列，以此作为整部作品的基础素材。这一方法为无调性音乐提供了逻辑秩序，深刻影响了此后半个世纪的严肃音乐创作。
contributionEn: Schoenberg was the foremost architect of a new order after the collapse of tonality. Recognizing that traditional tonal harmony had exhausted its possibilities, he refused to remain in the chaos of free atonality and instead invented the twelve-tone technique — arranging the twelve chromatic pitches of the octave into a fixed row that serves as the foundational material for an entire composition. This method gave atonal music a coherent logic and profoundly influenced serious composition for the next half century.
style: 勋伯格的创作横跨三个阶段：晚期浪漫主义（浓郁的瓦格纳式和声）、自由无调性（表现主义的极端情感张力）和十二音序列（严密的逻辑组织）。他的音乐以极高的表现力和紧张度著称，即使在最抽象的序列作品中也保持着强烈的情感投入。他的音乐要求听众全神贯注——没有重复、没有主旋律、每一个音都承载着结构功能。
styleEn: Schoenberg's output spans three distinct phases: late Romanticism (rich Wagnerian harmony), free atonality (the extreme emotional tension of Expressionism), and twelve-tone serialism (rigorous logical organization). His music is marked by extraordinary expressive intensity; even his most abstract serial works retain a fierce emotional commitment. It demands total concentration from the listener — there is no repetition, no singable tune, and every note carries structural weight.

famousScores.name: 《升华之夜》 Op.4
famousScores.desc: 弦乐六重奏（后改编为弦乐队版），晚期浪漫主义的绝唱
famousScores.descEn: A string sextet (later arranged for string orchestra) — a crowning achievement of late Romanticism
famousScores.detail:
《升华之夜》（1899）是勋伯格26岁时的作品，以理查·德梅尔的同名诗歌为蓝本：一对恋人在月夜中散步，女子坦白自己怀了另一个男人的孩子，男子以爱和宽恕接纳了她——月光将一切升华。这部弦乐六重奏将晚期浪漫主义的情感强度推向了极致，不协和和弦、半音和声、庞大的弦乐织体，将德梅尔的诗歌情感化为了纯粹的音响。

然而这部作品提交维也纳音乐协会时，却遭到了令人啼笑皆非的拒绝——评审委员会的理由是：乐谱中含有一个「无法解决的九和弦」（一个在当时的和声理论书籍中根本不存在的和弦），因此无法演出。勋伯格对此嘲讽地回应：「那么，也许应该等待音乐理论书籍承认这个和弦的存在之后，再来考虑是否演出。」这次被拒绝成为音乐史上最具讽刺意味的委员会决定之一。

作品于1902年首次公开演出，获得了热烈的成功，并成为勋伯格最受欢迎的作品，至今仍是音乐厅的常客。它像一座桥梁，连接着瓦格纳的浪漫晚期和20世纪的现代主义：既有对过去的深情告别，又暗示着即将到来的崭新世界。它也是勋伯格三个创作阶段中最容易被接受、最能打动普通听众的作品。
famousScores.detailEn:
Verklärte Nacht (1899) was composed when Schoenberg was twenty-six, based on Richard Dehmel's poem of the same name: a couple walking in moonlight; the woman confesses she is carrying another man's child; the man accepts her with love and forgiveness — the moonlight transfigures everything. This string sextet drives late-Romantic emotional intensity to its absolute limit: dissonant chords, chromatic harmony, and a vast string texture transform the poem's feelings into pure sound.

When the work was submitted to the Vienna Music Society, it was rejected on grounds that would be comic were they not so revealing: the adjudication committee cited the presence of an "unresolvable ninth chord" — a chord that, as it happened, did not appear in any harmony textbook of the day — and therefore could not be performed. Schoenberg responded with characteristic irony: "Perhaps it would be advisable to wait until the music theory books have caught up with this chord before considering whether to perform it." This rejection became one of the most ironically pointed committee decisions in music history.

The premiere in 1902 was warmly received, and the work has remained Schoenberg's most popular piece ever since, a regular presence in the concert hall. It stands as a bridge between Wagner's late Romanticism and twentieth-century modernism — a fond farewell to the past while hinting at the new world about to arrive. It is also, of all Schoenberg's works, the most accessible to general audiences.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Schoenberg Verklärte Nacht Herbert von Karajan Berlin Strings

famousScores.name: 《月迷彼埃罗》 Op.21
famousScores.desc: 21首音诗，使用「说唱」（Sprechstimme）技法，表现主义的标志
famousScores.descEn: Twenty-one poems employing Sprechstimme (speech-song), a landmark of musical Expressionism
famousScores.detail:
《月迷彼埃罗》（1912）是勋伯格自由无调性时期的巅峰，也是整个表现主义音乐的里程碑。作品以阿尔伯特·吉罗的法语诗歌（德语译本）为文本，21首音诗以5件室内乐器（长笛/短笛、单簧管/低音单簧管、小提琴/中提琴、大提琴、钢琴）和女声演唱，构成了一个关于月光、血与疯狂的噩梦幻想。

「说唱声乐」（Sprechstimme）是这部作品最革命性的创新：演唱者既不完全歌唱也不完全说话，而是用一种标注了大致音高走向的声调朗诵——音调在特定方向向上或向下弯曲，但不固定于某个音高，如同介于人声与乐器之间的幽灵。这种声音效果让许多最初的听众感到不安，甚至恐惧。

1912年10月16日在柏林首演，听众的反应两极化——喝彩声和嘘声同时响起。但作品在知识界和艺术界引发了深刻的讨论：斯特拉文斯基、拉威尔、布里顿都对其产生了强烈的反应（各不相同）。今天，《月迷彼埃罗》被普遍认为是音乐表现主义的核心文本，也是20世纪最重要的单部室内乐作品之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
Pierrot lunaire (1912) is the summit of Schoenberg's free-atonal period and a milestone of Expressionist music as a whole. Setting Albert Giraud's French poems (in German translation), twenty-one songs for five chamber instruments (flute/piccolo, clarinet/bass clarinet, violin/viola, cello, piano) and a female voice compose a nightmare fantasy of moonlight, blood, and madness.

Sprechstimme (speech-song) is the work's most revolutionary innovation: the vocalist neither sings fully nor speaks plainly but intones in a manner that indicates the approximate direction of pitch movement without fixing any particular note — the voice bends upward or downward as marked but floats above conventional pitch, like a ghost hovering between voice and instrument. This sonic effect left many early listeners deeply unsettled, even frightened.

At the Berlin premiere on October 16, 1912, the audience was violently divided — cheers and boos sounded simultaneously. Yet the work triggered profound discussion in intellectual and artistic circles: Stravinsky, Ravel, and Britten all responded to it intensely, each in their own way. Today Pierrot lunaire is universally regarded as the central text of musical Expressionism and one of the most important single chamber works of the twentieth century.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Schoenberg Pierrot lunaire Pierre Boulez Christine Schäfer

famousScores.name: 《华沙幸存者》 Op.46
famousScores.desc: 纳粹大屠杀的音乐纪念碑，撼人心魄
famousScores.descEn: A shattering musical memorial to the Holocaust
famousScores.detail:
《华沙幸存者》（1947）是勋伯格流亡美国后创作的最重要的单件作品，也是音乐文献中对纳粹大屠杀最直接、最有力的回应之一。以旁白者的英语旁白开始，讲述一个在华沙隔离区侥幸逃脱死亡的犹太人的亲历——德国士兵的铁靴声、点名、枪击——最终在全体合唱以希伯来语演唱《舍玛·以色列》（犹太祈祷文）时达到震撼人心的高潮。

勋伯格以十二音技法创作了这部只有约7分钟的短作，但其中蕴含的情感张力超越了许多长篇大作。他在创作时说：「我不是在创作音乐，我是在还债。」这部作品献给他所有在大屠杀中遇难的同族。首演于1948年11月4日，在新墨西哥州阿尔伯克基，完全出于偶然地由一个当地乐队首次演出。

《华沙幸存者》的历史意义超越了其音乐技法层面——它是20世纪最重要的「见证音乐」（Witness Music）之一，它证明了先锋音乐语言同样可以成为历史悲剧最直接的传声筒。它在以色列的音乐厅被长期演出，并成为大屠杀纪念活动的标准曲目。每次演出都是一次对历史的庄严复现。
famousScores.detailEn:
A Survivor from Warsaw (1947) is the most important single work Schoenberg composed during his American exile, and one of the most direct and powerful responses in the musical literature to the Holocaust. It opens with an English narration describing a Jewish man's escape from death in the Warsaw Ghetto — the tramp of German boots, the roll call, the gunfire — reaching a shattering climax when the full chorus sings the Hebrew prayer Shema Yisrael.

Schoenberg used the twelve-tone technique to compose this work of barely seven minutes, yet the emotional pressure it contains surpasses many far longer pieces. He said while composing it: "I am not creating music; I am paying a debt." The work is dedicated to all his people who perished in the genocide. Its premiere on November 4, 1948 in Albuquerque, New Mexico, came about almost accidentally through a local ensemble.

A Survivor from Warsaw transcends questions of musical technique — it is one of the most important works of "witness music" in the twentieth century, demonstrating that avant-garde musical language can serve as the most immediate transmitter of historical tragedy. It has long been performed regularly in Israeli concert halls and has become standard repertoire for Holocaust memorial events. Each performance is a solemn act of historical commemoration.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Schoenberg Survivor from Warsaw Pierre Boulez

famousScores.name: 《钢琴组曲》 Op.25
famousScores.desc: 第一部完全使用十二音技法的作品
famousScores.descEn: The first composition written entirely using the twelve-tone method
famousScores.detail:
《钢琴组曲》（1921—1923）是音乐史上第一部完整地将十二音技法贯穿始终的作品，因此具有无可争辩的历史里程碑意义。勋伯格以巴洛克组曲的体裁框架（前奏曲、加沃特舞曲、穆塞特舞曲、间奏曲、小步舞曲）来承载他全新的音乐语言，这一将最现代的技法嫁接到最古老的形式上的策略，体现了他一生中对于形式理性的执念。

整部组曲使用同一个十二音序列（E—F—G—降D—升G—降E—降A—D—B—C—升A—降B）的不同变形形式（原形、逆行、倒影、逆行倒影）构建而成。尽管技法严密，这部作品并非冰冷的理论练习——其中不乏活泼的节奏和音乐幽默（加沃特舞曲部分甚至带有一种巴洛克式的机巧），显示勋伯格在发明新体系的同时，从未放弃对音乐乐趣的追求。

这部组曲对后来半个世纪的严肃音乐创作影响深远——它向全世界的作曲家展示了十二音技法作为一种创作方法的可行性与灵活性。韦伯恩、贝尔格以及后来的布列兹，都从这部作品中找到了各自发展方向的起点。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Suite for Piano (1921–23) is the first composition in music history to apply the twelve-tone technique consistently throughout, and its place as a historical landmark is therefore uncontested. Schoenberg used the framework of the Baroque suite (Prelude, Gavotte, Musette, Intermezzo, Minuet) to house his entirely new musical language — a strategy of grafting the most modern technique onto the oldest forms that reflects his lifelong obsession with formal rationality.

The entire work is constructed from the transformations (original, retrograde, inversion, retrograde inversion) of a single twelve-tone row (E—F—G—D-flat—G-sharp—E-flat—A-flat—D—B—C—A-sharp—B-flat). Despite its technical rigor, the work is not a cold theoretical exercise — there is plenty of lively rhythm and musical wit (the Gavotte has an almost Baroque playfulness), demonstrating that Schoenberg never abandoned the pursuit of musical pleasure while inventing his new system.

The Suite exerted a deep influence on serious composition for the next half century, demonstrating to composers worldwide the feasibility and flexibility of the twelve-tone method as a compositional approach. Webern, Berg, and later Boulez all found in this work the starting point for their own divergent paths.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Schoenberg Piano Suite Op.25 Maurizio Pollini

famousScores.name: 《五首管弦乐曲》 Op.16
famousScores.desc: 自由无调性的管弦乐杰作，探索音色旋律
famousScores.descEn: A masterpiece of free atonality exploring Klangfarbenmelodie — melody made of shifting tone colors
famousScores.detail:
《五首管弦乐曲》（1909）是勋伯格自由无调性时期最重要的管弦乐作品，也是整个表现主义音乐的代表之作。五首作品各有其性格：《预感》《过去》《夏晨湖畔的色彩》《插曲》《强迫性》，构成了一幅抽象的内心风景画卷。其中第三首《夏晨湖畔的色彩》是整个音乐史上「音色旋律」（Klangfarbenmelodie）理念的最著名体现。

「音色旋律」是勋伯格提出的一个核心概念：旋律不再由单一乐器演奏，而是由一个和弦在不同乐器之间以极慢的速度转换，每次转换都改变和弦的音色，但音高保持不变。结果是音乐中产生了一种微妙的颜色流动，如同光线在水面上的渐变。这一理念后来深刻影响了频谱音乐等20世纪后半期的音乐流派。

这部作品1912年在伦敦首演，指挥是亨利·伍德（Henry Wood），最初演出为缩减版（以避免争议），后来的完整版首演在柏林。作品在首演时引发了强烈争议，但指挥家如今普遍将其视为现代管弦乐文献的重要基石，证明了交响乐在放弃调性之后依然可以构建强大的音乐建筑。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Five Pieces for Orchestra (1909) is Schoenberg's most important orchestral work from his free-atonal period and a definitive example of Expressionist music. The five pieces — "Premonitions," "The Past," "Summer Morning by a Lake," "Peripeteia," and "The Obligatory Recitative" — compose an abstract panorama of the inner landscape. The third piece, "Summer Morning by a Lake," is the most celebrated embodiment in all of music history of the concept of Klangfarbenmelodie.

Klangfarbenmelodie (tone-color melody) is a core concept Schoenberg articulated: rather than being played by a single instrument, a melody (or chord) passes with extreme slowness among different instruments, each handoff altering the timbre while the pitch remains the same. The result is a subtle flow of color through the music, like the changing play of light across water. This idea later profoundly influenced spectral music and other movements of the second half of the twentieth century.

The work was premiered in London in 1912 under Henry Wood in a reduced version (to minimize controversy); the complete version premiered later in Berlin. It caused intense controversy on first performance, but conductors today generally regard it as an important cornerstone of the modern orchestral literature, demonstrating that symphonic music could still build powerful architecture after the abandonment of tonality.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Schoenberg Five Orchestral Pieces Op.16 Pierre Boulez

famousScores.name: 《古雷之歌》 Gurrelieder
famousScores.desc: 后浪漫主义的庞大史诗，需要五位独唱、三个男声合唱团、一个混声合唱团和巨型管弦乐队
famousScores.descEn: A colossal late-Romantic epic requiring five soloists, four choruses, and a massive orchestra
famousScores.detail:
《古雷之歌》（1900—1903年构思，1911年才最终配器完成）是勋伯格最宏大的作品，也是20世纪音乐史上规模最大的单件作品之一。乐谱需要8支长笛、5支双簧管、7支单簧管、5支巴松管、10支圆号、6支小号、6支长号、4架竖琴……仅是乐谱手稿就厚达数百页，以至于勋伯格不得不专门定制特大号的五线纸。

作品改编自丹麦诗人雅各布森（Jens Peter Jacobsen）的德语版史诗诗集，讲述中世纪丹麦国王瓦尔德马尔与他挚爱的托维如何在宗教与世俗的冲突中走向悲剧。这部作品创作于勋伯格走向无调性之前，因此它代表了他的晚期浪漫主义顶峰——那种浓郁的瓦格纳式和声在这里获得了近乎贪婪的最后燃烧。

1913年2月23日在维也纳的首演是一场传奇性的音乐盛事，观众席的欢呼声长达15分钟，在勋伯格的生涯中极为罕见——他的大多数作品都在首演时引起争议而非掌声。这次成功的讽刺在于：此时的勋伯格已经完全走向了无调性，而观众们狂热鼓掌的却是他即将抛弃的那种风格。
famousScores.detailEn:
Gurrelieder (conceived 1900–03; final orchestration completed 1911) is Schoenberg's most monumental work and one of the largest single pieces in twentieth-century music history. The score calls for eight flutes, five oboes, seven clarinets, five bassoons, ten horns, six trumpets, six trombones, four harps... the manuscript runs to hundreds of pages and Schoenberg had to commission specially oversized music paper to accommodate it.

The work is adapted from the Danish poet Jens Peter Jacobsen's epic poetry cycle (in German translation), telling of the tragic love between medieval Danish King Waldemar and his beloved Tove, torn apart by the conflict between religion and earthly desire. It was composed before Schoenberg moved toward atonality and represents the absolute summit of his late-Romantic phase — that dense Wagnerian harmony burns here with an almost voracious final intensity.

The Vienna premiere on February 23, 1913 was a legendary musical event; the audience's ovation lasted fifteen minutes — extraordinarily rare for a Schoenberg premiere, most of which provoked controversy rather than applause. The irony was exquisite: by this point Schoenberg had long since moved beyond tonality entirely, and the audience was applauding wildly for a style he had already left behind.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Schoenberg Gurrelieder Pierre Boulez BBC Symphony

famousScores.name: 《第二弦乐四重奏》 Op.10（含女高音）
famousScores.desc: 历史性的转折点——最后一个乐章放弃了调性，迈向自由无调性
famousScores.descEn: A historical turning point — the final movement abandons tonality, stepping into free atonality
famousScores.detail:
《第二弦乐四重奏》（1907—1908）是勋伯格的重要转折点，在音乐史上具有划时代的意义。前两个乐章仍在调性范围内写作，但后两个乐章加入了女高音演唱斯蒂芬·格奥尔格（Stefan George）的诗歌，和声语言越来越模糊调性中心。第四乐章开头的诗句「我感到来自另一个星球的空气」（Ich fühle luft von anderem planeten），被后世公认为勋伯格宣告调性崩溃的历史时刻。

这部四重奏在维也纳首演时引发了轩然大波——这也是音乐史上最著名的「恶意哄场」之一。观众席上有人大声哄闹，有人发出嘘声，甚至有人故意发出鼓掌声来打断演出。曾有记载称：开始时观众以为笑声和发噪音是在开玩笑，后来才意识到这是真实的演出骚乱。身处其中的指挥家古斯塔夫·马勒（Gustav Mahler）被激怒了，据说他站起来要求制止骚动。

这部四重奏标志着整个西方音乐史的一个真正的断裂点——从有调性到无调性。那句「我感到来自另一个星球的空气」，今天听来既是诗歌的表达，也是历史的预言：那个「另一个星球」就是勋伯格即将进入的无调性世界。
famousScores.detailEn:
The String Quartet No. 2 (1907–08) is a pivotal turning point in Schoenberg's career and an epoch-making moment in music history. The first two movements remain within the tonal sphere, but the third and fourth add a soprano singing poems by Stefan George, with the harmonic language growing progressively more dissolved. The opening line of the fourth movement — "I feel the air of another planet" (Ich fühle luft von anderem planeten) — is universally recognized as the historical moment Schoenberg announced the collapse of tonality.

The Viennese premiere provoked a major scandal — one of the most famous cases of deliberate disruption in music history. Audience members shouted, hissed, and made deliberate applause to interrupt the performance. Gustav Mahler was in the hall and reportedly stood up in fury, demanding silence. Some accounts describe how the initial disruption was mistaken for joking before it became clear the uproar was genuine.

This quartet marks a genuine rupture in the entire history of Western music — the passage from tonality to atonality. "I feel the air of another planet," heard today, sounds simultaneously like poetic utterance and historical prophecy: that "other planet" is the atonal world Schoenberg was about to enter.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Schoenberg String Quartet No.2 Juilliard Quartet

famousScores.name: 《第一室内交响曲》 Op.9
famousScores.desc: 15位独奏演奏家，一部精心压缩的交响世界，调性崩溃前的极限
famousScores.descEn: Fifteen solo players, one meticulously compressed symphonic universe — tonality pushed to its breaking point
famousScores.detail:
《第一室内交响曲》（1906）是勋伯格在迈向无调性之前最后一部坚守在（极端）调性框架内的大型作品。他将传统四乐章交响曲的结构压缩为一个单一乐章，同时将演奏编制从大型管弦乐队缩减为15位独奏演奏家——长笛、双簧管、英国管、降E调单簧管、A调单簧管、降B调单簧管、低音单簧管、巴松管、倍低音巴松管、两支圆号，以及弦乐五重奏。这种精密的室内乐质感，与其宏大的音乐野心形成了极度紧张的对比。

作品的和声语言已经被推到了调性的极限边缘：全音阶片段、四度叠置的和弦构建等技法，预示着即将到来的无调性突破。同时，作品的结构却极为严密——主题、发展部、再现部和赋格段落都以高度精简的方式被组织在一个单一乐章中，如同一个被高度压缩的交响世界。

这部作品在1906年首演时受到了强烈的否定——维也纳批评界将其描述为「音乐的噪音」。但今天，它被视为20世纪音乐最重要的过渡性作品之一，是在调性即将崩溃前最后一刻的精彩挣扎，也是室内乐与交响乐融合的先驱性尝试。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Chamber Symphony No. 1 (1906) is the last large-scale work Schoenberg held within — however extreme — a tonal framework before crossing into atonality. He compressed the traditional four-movement symphonic structure into a single movement and reduced the performing forces from a full orchestra to fifteen solo players: flute, oboe, English horn, three clarinets, bass clarinet, bassoon, contrabassoon, two horns, and a string quintet. The intimate chamber texture this creates is in extreme tension with the music's vast symphonic ambitions.

The harmonic language is already pushed to the very edge of tonality: whole-tone passages, quartal harmony, and other devices foreshadow the atonal breakthrough now just around the corner. At the same time, the structure is immaculately tight — themes, development, recapitulation, and fugal passages are organized in a single movement at a degree of compression that suggests a whole symphonic world forced into a dense nugget.

The premiere in 1906 provoked fierce condemnation — Viennese critics described it as "musical noise." Today it is regarded as one of the most important transitional works of twentieth-century music: a brilliant final struggle at the moment before tonality's collapse, and a pioneering attempt to fuse the chamber and symphonic traditions.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Schoenberg Chamber Symphony No.1 Pierre Boulez

famousScores.name: 《管弦乐变奏曲》 Op.31
famousScores.desc: 十二音技法在大型管弦乐队上的首次展示，也是最成功的展示
famousScores.descEn: The twelve-tone method's first deployment in full orchestra — and its most successful
famousScores.detail:
《管弦乐变奏曲》（1926—1928）是勋伯格将十二音技法第一次运用于大型管弦乐队的作品，也是他最雄心勃勃的序列音乐创作。作品由一个主题和九个变奏构成，最后以尾声结束，整体结构呼应着传统变奏曲体裁，但在调性语言上已经完全跨入了无调性的世界。

这部变奏曲的管弦乐配器展现了勋伯格在技法上的高超掌控——尽管十二音序列的严格运用给旋律写作带来了极大的限制，勋伯格仍然创造出了极富个性的音色对比和音乐表情。变奏主题由大提琴独奏呈示，之后的九个变奏各有其鲜明的性格：有的如鬼魅般轻盈，有的充满戏剧性的紧张，有的（第九变奏）甚至引用了巴赫的《科里雍达》（Coriolano）主题。

1928年12月2日由福特文格勒指挥柏林爱乐首演，反应冷淡，甚至有人鼓倒彩。但这部作品今天被认为是十二音音乐最重要的管弦乐范本，也是分析勋伯格序列技法最完整的文本之一，在世界各地的音乐学院中被广泛用于教学，尽管在音乐厅中的上演频率仍然相对有限。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Variations for Orchestra (1926–28) is the first work in which Schoenberg applied the twelve-tone technique to a full symphony orchestra, and his most ambitious serial composition. It comprises a theme, nine variations, and a finale, the overall structure echoing the traditional variation form while the tonal language has crossed entirely into atonality.

The orchestration displays Schoenberg's supreme command of technique — despite the severe constraints that the twelve-tone row places on melodic writing, he creates vividly individual timbral contrasts and musical expressions throughout. The theme is stated by a solo cello; the nine variations are sharply differentiated in character: some ghostly and weightless, some intensely dramatic, and the ninth even quotes Bach.

Wilhelm Furtwängler conducted the Berlin Philharmonic premiere on December 2, 1928 to a cool response — there were boos. Yet the work today is regarded as the most important orchestral model of twelve-tone music and one of the most complete texts for analyzing Schoenberg's serial technique, widely used for teaching at music schools worldwide, even if concert performances remain relatively infrequent.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Schoenberg Variations Orchestra Op.31 Boulez Berlin

famousScores.name: 《摩西与亚伦》（歌剧）
famousScores.desc: 勋伯格一生最宏大的未竟之作，关于言语与沉默、神性与人性的哲学歌剧
famousScores.descEn: Schoenberg's grandest unfinished work — a philosophical opera on speech, silence, and the divine
famousScores.detail:
《摩西与亚伦》（1930—1932年作，第三幕从未完成）是勋伯格一生中最宏大、也最令人心碎的未竟之业。他几乎用了二十年构思这部歌剧，最终只完成了两幕——第三幕留下了文字草稿，但从未配上音乐。关于他为何无法完成第三幕，勋伯格本人只说：「我会在完成之前死去。」他说对了。

歌剧以《圣经》中摩西（Moses）和亚伦（Aaron）的兄弟对比为核心：摩西代表抽象的神性真理——他的角色只能「说话」（以一种接近说唱声乐的特殊念白演唱）而无法歌唱，因为真理超越了可表达的语言；亚伦代表言语的美与诱惑——他的角色用美声歌唱，因为他是向人民传达神圣信息的使者。两兄弟之间的冲突，是艺术家与世俗之间、真理与可交流性之间永恒张力的寓言。

这部歌剧完全用十二音序列写成，是这一技法在歌剧上最雄心勃勃的运用。1954年在汉堡的两幕版首演，令所有人震惊——它所触碰的关于「艺术家能否将纯粹的精神真理传达给大众」的问题，在勋伯格死后三年被搬上舞台，比任何完整的答案都更加令人深思。
famousScores.detailEn:
Moses und Aron (composed 1930–32; the third act was never completed) is the grandest and most heartbreaking unfinished project of Schoenberg's life. He spent nearly twenty years conceiving the opera and finished only two acts — the third act exists as a text sketch but was never set to music. Asked why he could not complete the third act, Schoenberg replied simply: "I will die before I finish it." He was right.

The opera centers on the biblical contrast between Moses and Aaron: Moses represents abstract divine truth — his role can only "speak" (in a specialized half-sung, half-spoken Sprechstimme) and never sing, because truth transcends expressible language; Aaron represents the beauty and seduction of speech — his role sings in bel canto, for he is the messenger who conveys divine truth to the people. The conflict between the brothers is an allegory of the eternal tension between the artist and the world, between truth and communicability.

The opera is written entirely in twelve-tone technique, the most ambitious application of the method in the operatic medium. The premiere of the two-act version in Hamburg in 1954 — three years after Schoenberg's death — left audiences stunned; the question it raises about whether any artist can convey pure spiritual truth to a mass audience is, in the absence of a completed third act, more profoundly open than any completed answer could have been.

influence: 勋伯格的十二音技法通过他的学生韦伯恩和贝尔格（合称「第二维也纳乐派」）传播开来，并在二战后被布列兹、施托克豪森等人发展为全面序列主义。斯特拉文斯基晚年也转向了序列技法。尽管十二音音乐从未赢得广大听众的喜爱，它作为一种思维方式深刻影响了20世纪音乐教育和作曲理论。他提出的「音色旋律」（Klangfarbenmelodie）概念启发了频谱音乐等后续流派。
influenceEn: Schoenberg's twelve-tone method spread through his pupils Webern and Berg — together known as the Second Viennese School — and was extended into total serialism after World War II by Boulez, Stockhausen, and others. Even Stravinsky adopted serial techniques in his late works. Although twelve-tone music never won a mass audience, as an intellectual framework it profoundly shaped twentieth-century music education and compositional theory. His concept of Klangfarbenmelodie (tone-color melody) went on to inspire spectral music and other later movements.
